verses.text: David asks God to judge and examine him, for he has walked in integrity and trusted the LORD without wavering. He welcomes the divine test. A sincere heart does not fear God's scrutiny but invites it.

David asks God to judge and examine him, for he has walked in integrity and trusted the LORD without wavering. He welcomes the divine test. A sincere heart does not fear God's scrutiny but invites it.

David asks God to judge and examine him, for he has walked in integrity and trusted the LORD without wavering. He welcomes the divine test. A sincere heart does not fear God's scrutiny but invites it.

He has not sat with the deceitful nor joined the assembly of evildoers, but hated their company. He guards his fellowships. Whom we keep company with shapes the soul, so the godly choose their companions with care.

He has not sat with the deceitful nor joined the assembly of evildoers, but hated their company. He guards his fellowships. Whom we keep company with shapes the soul, so the godly choose their companions with care.
He washes his hands in innocence to come about God's altar, declaring, 'I have loved the habitation of thy house.' Worship is his joy. The heart right with God delights to be where His glory dwells.
He washes his hands in innocence to come about God's altar, declaring, 'I have loved the habitation of thy house.' Worship is his joy. The heart right with God delights to be where His glory dwells.
He washes his hands in innocence to come about God's altar, declaring, 'I have loved the habitation of thy house.' Worship is his joy. The heart right with God delights to be where His glory dwells.
He prays not to be swept away with sinners whose hands are full of bribes and mischief. He dreads sharing their doom. To love righteousness is to long not to share the fate of the wicked.
He prays not to be swept away with sinners whose hands are full of bribes and mischief. He dreads sharing their doom. To love righteousness is to long not to share the fate of the wicked.

'But as for me, I will walk in mine integrity,' he resolves, his feet on level ground, ready to bless the LORD. Grace upholds his stand. The redeemed walk uprightly, not to earn mercy, but as those already kept by it.
